summary: Matched several trophy-list functions and advanced the unmatched trophy-list initializer by refining tylist structures, moving shared toy globals into the common ty types header, and using matching-sensitive source rewrites such as string-table offsets, array fields for repeated links/JObjs/texts, adjusted return types, and stack padding.
what_changed: Added a full C implementation for previously placeholder `un_80313774`, covering trophy-list state initialization, circular entry linking, JObj/text creation, selection setup, and initial scrolling/positioning behavior.
Matched `un_803124BC` and `un_8031263C` by replacing literal archive/symbol strings such as `TyDatai.dat`, `TyDatai.usd`, and `tyInitModelTbl` with offsets from `un_803FDD18`.

Changed `un_8031305C` return type from `s8` to `s32` in both `tylist.c` and `tylist.h`, adjusted its stack padding to `PAD_STACK(0xC)`, and introduced a boolean-like local assignment before the main movement branch.
Refined `TyListArg` from anonymous offset fields (`x0`, `x4`, `xC`, `x10`, `x14`) into grouped arrays: `links[3]`, `jobjs[3]`, and `texts[3]`, with callsites updated accordingly.
Extended `TyListState` with explicit `x2C2` and padding around offset `0x2C2`, allowing code to use `state->x2C2` instead of raw byte indexing.
Added `TyListWaitData` in `tylist.c` so `fn_8031438C` can write `x20` and `x24` instead of using raw integer pointer casts.

Reworked `un_80313508` to use `HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z` and a simpler `if (joint == NULL) OSPanic(...) else ...` structure, while preserving parent-child JObj insertion and standalone GObj creation paths.
Moved duplicated toy-related local typedefs from `toy.c` into `types.h`, consolidating several partial `ToyGlobals*_` views and `TyUnk25` usage into a broader `ToyGlobalsS_` plus `ToySubStructS_`.
Updated `toy.c` to use the shared `ToyGlobalsS_` type for `un_804D6E68` and related local variables instead of multiple narrow local struct aliases.
Adjusted `un_803147C4` to operate through `char* data` and explicit offsets for `memzero`, plus temporary `HSD_GObj**` and `LightList**` variables for matching.
smart_moves: Grouping repeated struct members into arrays (`links`, `jobjs`, `texts`) both clarified the circular list/JObj relationships and produced cleaner matching callsites where the original code likely indexed contiguous fields.
Using a single expanded `ToyGlobalsS_` in `types.h` avoided several overlapping local typedefs in `toy.c`, making cross-file toy state access reusable for future ty/toy work.
Replacing hardcoded string literals with `data + offset` references from `un_803FDD18` was a targeted matching tactic for functions that load archive symbols through a shared string/data block.

Keeping the `un_80313508` behavior expressed through existing HSD setter helpers avoided manually reproducing JObj dirty-flag/assert code and helped bring the function to 100% according to the bot report.
Introducing small local structs like `TyListWaitData` reduced raw pointer arithmetic in `fn_8031438C` without requiring full semantic naming of the object yet.
The circular linked-list setup in `un_80313774` made later replacements from `x0`/`x4` to `links[0]`/`links[1]` consistent across movement, paging, and selection code.
decomp_lessons: When several fields repeat at fixed stride, consider an array in the struct instead of naming each offset individually; this can align better with compiler indexing and improve readability.
For decompilation, a broader shared struct can be preferable to multiple narrow overlay structs when the same global object is accessed at many offsets across files.
String references may need to be expressed as offsets into an existing data blob rather than as C string literals to match codegen and relocation behavior.
Return type corrections matter for matching: changing `un_8031305C` from `s8` to `s32` was part of the final-match set reported by the bot.
Do not assume direct field writes are best for JObj transforms; using HSD helper setters can match compiler output while hiding complex inline dirty-flag behavior.
Explicit small structs for otherwise unknown callback/wait data can improve matching and future comprehension even when field names remain offset-based.

A code-match improvement can still regress a data section: the bot reported `.data` fell from 20.07% to 4.40%, likely related to string/data layout changes, so code and data matching should be tracked separately.
naming_conventions: `TyListArg.links[0]` and `links[1]` replaced previous `x0`/`x4` next/previous-style pointers; the evidence suggests these are circular list links, but exact semantic direction should still be confirmed.
`TyListArg.jobjs[0..2]` replaced individual JObj fields and is used for entry root/child/model-related JObjs in trophy-list rendering.
`TyListArg.texts[0..2]` documents three contiguous text handles created per trophy-list entry.
`TyListWaitData` follows the local unknown-struct convention with offset fields (`x20`, `x24`) while eliminating raw casts.
`ToyGlobalsS_` and `ToySubStructS_` remain underscore-suffixed unknown/shared ty/toy structs, but now live in `types.h` for reuse.
The PR keeps many unresolved symbols as `un_803...`/`un_804...`, so semantic naming evidence is limited.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(0xC)` in `un_8031305C` and `PAD_STACK(0x18)`/`PAD_STACK(8)` in nearby functions to control stack layout.
Introduced `i = ((s8) state->x29F) > 0; if (i) { ... }` in `un_8031305C`, a source-shape tweak likely intended to match condition/codegen.
Replaced string literals passed to `lbArchive_LoadSymbols` with `char* data = un_803FDD18` plus fixed offsets such as `0x608`, `0x614`, `0x9DC`, `0xA58`, and related symbol-name offsets.
Used `char* data = un_804A2AC0` plus offset-based `memzero(data + 0x2AC, 0x18)` and `memzero(data, 0x2AC)` in `un_803147C4` instead of fully typed field access.
Used helper calls `HSD_JObjSetTranslateX`, `HSD_JObjSetTranslateY`, and `HSD_JObjSetTranslateZ` instead of direct `jobj->translate` writes to avoid expanding dirty matrix logic.
Added casts and temporaries such as `HSD_GObj** gobj`, `LightList** jobj`, `HSD_Text* new_var`, and `(double) 2` to influence generated code in `un_803147C4`/`fn_8031438C`.
Returned the last child after `HSD_JObjAddChild` by walking `parentJobj->child` through `next`, preserving the observed child insertion result in `un_80313508`.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code improved to 67.24%, a +0.07% increase and +2552 bytes.
Bot reported 5 new 100% matches in `main/melee/ty/tylist`: `un_8031263C`, `fn_8031438C`, `un_8031305C`, `un_80313508`, and `un_803124BC`.
Bot reported `un_80313774` improved from 0.00% to 62.48%, `.sdata2` from 12.90% to 37.84%, `un_803147C4` from 78.58% to 85.97%, and `fn_80313BD8` slightly from 95.22% to 95.34%.
Bot reported a `.data` regression from 20.07% to 4.40%.
follow_up_queries: Investigate why `.data` matching regressed after replacing tylist string literals with `un_803FDD18` offsets.
Continue matching the remaining 37.52% of `un_80313774` and use its circular-list setup to assign semantic names to `links[0]` and `links[1]`.
Confirm the semantic roles of `TyListArg.jobjs[0]`, `jobjs[1]`, and `jobjs[2]` from trophy model/icon rendering behavior.
Refine `ToyGlobalsS_` field names now that it combines offsets used by toy and tylist code.
Check whether `TyListWaitData` corresponds to an existing HSD/GObj user data type elsewhere in the codebase.
